title: Starry Night
description: A swirling night sky over a village, painted during van Gogh's stay at the Saint-Paul-de-Mausole asylum in Saint-Rémy-de-Provence.

title: Girl with a Pearl Earring
description: Often referred to as the 'Mona Lisa of the North,' this painting depicts a girl wearing an exotic dress and a large pearl earring.

title: The Great Wave off Kanagawa
description: A dramatic depiction of a towering wave threatening boats near the coast of Kanagawa, with Mount Fuji visible in the background.

title: The Persistence of Memory
description: Melting pocket watches in a dreamlike landscape, one of the most recognizable works of Surrealism.

title: The Birth of Venus
description: Venus, the goddess of love, emerges from the sea as a fully grown woman, arriving at the shore.

title: Nighthawks
description: Four figures sit in a brightly lit downtown diner late at night, viewed through a large glass window from the dark street outside.

title: Water Lilies
description: Part of Monet's famous series depicting his flower garden at Giverny, focusing on the reflections and colors of the water surface.

title: The Kiss
medium: Oil and gold leaf on canvas
description: A couple locked in an intimate embrace, wrapped in elaborate gold-patterned robes, set against a gold background.

title: Guernica
description: A powerful anti-war statement responding to the bombing of the Basque town of Guernica during the Spanish Civil War.

title: The Scream
medium: Oil, tempera, and pastel on cardboard
description: A figure stands on a bridge with an agonized expression against a turbulent orange sky.

title: Mona Lisa
description: The world's most famous painting depicts a seated woman with an enigmatic smile, believed to be Lisa Gherardini. Leonardo's mastery of sfumato gives the subject her mysterious, atmospheric quality.

title: Las Meninas
description: A complex and enigmatic composition showing the Infanta Margarita surrounded by her ladies-in-waiting, with Velázquez himself visible painting at a large canvas. The work plays with mirrors, perspective, and the nature of representation.

title: The Last Supper
description: Painted on the refectory wall of a Milan convent, this monumental mural captures the dramatic moment Christ announces that one of his apostles will betray him, capturing twelve distinct emotional reactions.

title: American Gothic
description: One of the most recognizable images in 20th-century American art, depicting a farmer and his daughter standing before a wooden house. Wood used his sister and dentist as models for the austere, stoic figures.

title: A Sunday on La Grande Jatte
description: Seurat's monumental pointillist masterpiece depicts Parisians relaxing on an island in the Seine. Composed of millions of tiny dots of color, it took two years to complete and founded the Neo-Impressionist movement.

title: Wanderer above the Sea of Fog
description: A lone figure stands on a rocky precipice, gazing out over a vast landscape shrouded in mist. The quintessential Romantic image of solitary contemplation in the face of sublime nature.

title: Impression, Sunrise
description: The painting that gave Impressionism its name. Monet's loose, spontaneous brushwork captures the shimmer of morning light over the port of Le Havre, with an orange sun reflected on the misty water.

title: The Arnolfini Portrait
description: A double portrait of an Italian merchant and his wife, renowned for its meticulous detail and symbolic richness. The convex mirror in the background reflects two additional figures, one of whom may be van Eyck himself.

title: The Hay Wain
description: A tranquil scene of rural English life on the River Stour, with a horse-drawn cart fording the water beside Willy Lott's cottage. Constable's naturalistic depiction of the Suffolk landscape was revolutionary for its time.

title: Le Déjeuner sur l'herbe
description: Manet's scandalous submission to the 1863 Salon des Refusés shocked audiences with its juxtaposition of a nude woman and two fully clothed men in a contemporary outdoor setting, breaking sharply with academic tradition.
